title: Cooperative matrix via SPV_KHR_cooperative_matrix
goal: Add portable tensor-core access via SPV_KHR_cooperative_matrix (promoted in Vulkan 1.3.300). This is THE defining AXIOM-Compute feature — the point where the project transcends CPU-compiler parity and delivers on the 'AI-first GPU IR' thesis. Portable across NVIDIA Turing+, AMD RDNA3+, Intel Arc.
scope_in: New type: matrix[T, M, N, use] where T in {f16, f32, i8, u8, i32, u32} and use in {'a', 'b', 'accumulator'}
- coopmat_load(buffer, offset, stride) -> matrix[T, M, N, use]
- coopmat_mul_add(a: matrix[_, M, K, 'a'], b: matrix[_, K, N, 'b'], c: matrix[_, M, N, 'accumulator']) -> matrix[_, M, N, 'accumulator']
- coopmat_zero() -> matrix[T, M, N, use] (initialize accumulator to zero)
Annotation: @cooperative_matrix(M, N, K, A_type, B_type, C_type) hints codegen + validates dimensions
Required SPIR-V extension: SPV_KHR_cooperative_matrix (emitted via OpExtension when used)
New HIR types: Ty::CoopMatrix { elem: ScalarTy, m: u32, n: u32, use: CoopMatUse }

Dimension constraints enforced at HIR: M*N*K tile shape must match SPIR-V spec's supported shapes (per target hardware query at runtime — for M2.1 just validate shape is positive and accept any combo; runtime preflight to M2.2+)
Example: examples/matmul_tile.axc — small tile matmul (16x16x16 f16 x f16 -> f32) using cooperative matrix
Compile + spirv-val integration test; Lavapipe dispatch deferred (software Vulkan lacks cooperative matrix hardware)
CI: compile-only test passes; runtime GPU test is GPU-gated with 'cooperative_matrix_required' flag — skip on Lavapipe, run on NVIDIA/AMD/Intel self-hosted runners (future)
scope_out: Runtime dispatch test on real tensor-core hardware (deferred until GPU CI available)
Matrix types as kernel parameters (they're function-local only for M2.1)
Matrix-to-matrix element access (no coopmat[i,j] indexing; only through loads/stores)
acceptance_criteria: Emitted SPIR-V contains OpExtension SPV_KHR_cooperative_matrix + OpCapability CooperativeMatrixKHR when matrix type used
matrix type on kernel-scope let binding only; rejected as kernel param type in M2.1 (future milestone)
Backward compat: all M0..M1.5 tests (426 baseline) pass, all 6 GPU tests on Lavapipe pass
open_questions_for_architect: Matrix type as let binding vs kernel parameter? M2.1 scope: let binding only. Parameter support in M3.
coopmat_mul_add: how does the type system enforce that the K dimensions match between a and b? And that the accumulator C matches a's M and b's N? Requires type-level reasoning.
CoopMatUse: 'a', 'b', 'accumulator' — represent as enum or string? Pick enum per anti-pattern #7.
Scope for load/store: SPIR-V has Subgroup, Workgroup scope for coopmat load/store. Default Subgroup scope for M2.1.
Supported dimensions: Vulkan spec requires drivers to expose supported M/N/K triples via VkCooperativeMatrixPropertiesKHR. M2.1 doesn't preflight — any positive M,N,K compiles. Runtime may reject.
Element stride: coopmat_load takes stride (elements, not bytes); document.
F16 primitive type: AXIOM-Compute currently supports f16 in type system? If not, add — cooperative matrix is typically f16 inputs.
Matrix layout: RowMajor vs ColumnMajor — M2.1 hardcodes RowMajor; add as enum parameter in M2.2.
